title: useFetch Hook
description:
Implement a **useFetch** custom hook that fetches data from a URL and manages loading, error, and data states.

### Requirements

- `useFetch<T>(url: string)` returns `{ data: T | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null, refetch: () => void }`
- On mount (and when `url` changes), fetch the URL and parse JSON
- Set `loading` to `true` while fetching, `false` when complete
- On success, set `data` to the parsed JSON and `error` to `null`
- On failure, set `error` to the error message and `data` to `null`
- `refetch()` triggers a fresh fetch (re-runs the request)
- Cancel in-flight requests when the URL changes or on unmount (AbortController)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Loading indicator | `loading` |
| Error message | `error` |
| Data display | `data` |
| Refetch button | `refetch` |
constraints:
- Implement in useFetch.ts as a standalone hook
- Use the native fetch API (no axios or other libraries)
- Use AbortController for cancellation
- Must handle the component unmounting during a pending fetch
hints: Create an AbortController at the start of your effect and pass its signal to fetch().
Use a state object or separate useState calls for data, loading, and error.
The refetch function can increment a counter in state to re-trigger the useEffect.
When catching errors, check if the error is an AbortError and ignore it.
